1080P + 120° GENİŞ AÇI + Eller serbest POV: 1920 x 1080P yüksek çözünürlük ve 120° geniş açı ile bu su geçirmez kask kamerası daha geniş bir görüş alanı yakalamak için net ve keskin görüntüler sunar. Eller serbest POV modu, eller serbest video çekimini destekler ve ister aileniz ve çocuklarınızla eğlenin, evcil hayvanlarınızla oynayın veya arkadaşlarınızla yolda olun, güzel anları kaydetmeyi kolaylaştırır. SAĞLAM SU GEÇİRMEZ TASARIM: Benzersiz bir dalış gövdesi ile donatılmış 360 aksiyon kamerası normalde su basıncından korkmadan 30 metre derinlikte kayıt yapabilir. Can it record in low light?

Rufus is generating a response...
The Meoilkolnm Mini Body Camera has automatic exposure control and works in both indoor and outdoor conditions, but it does have limitations in low light conditions.

Based on customer reviews, some users mentioned that it "lacks backlight making dark videos," which suggests the camera struggles in very low light situations. The camera uses a CMOS sensor with automatic settings, which helps in moderate lighting but may not perform as well as dedicated night vision cameras in very dark environments.

Does it have night vision?

Rufus is generating a response...
No, the Meoilkolnm Mini Body Camera does not have night vision capability.

According to customer Q&A responses, multiple users confirm "No night vision" and "Unfortunately it doesn't." However, they note it works well in city lighting at night but "not at all" in rooms with no light.

The camera uses automatic exposure control and a CMOS sensor, which helps in moderate lighting conditions but lacks the infrared LEDs needed for true night vision recording.

Is it easy to use?

Rufus is generating a response...
Yes, the Meoilkolnm Mini Body Camera is very easy to use. Customers consistently praise its simplicity:

Simple one-button design - Just press the middle button to start recording
Indicator light - Shows when recording so you don't miss any moments
Quick setup - No complicated configurations needed
Multiple mounting options - Magnetic pendant, safety cord, and clip for versatile placement
Reviews highlight that it's "super easy to use, just hold the button to turn it on or off. Simple and easy" and works great for both beginners and professionals. The camera functions well without requiring the app - you can simply press record and transfer files via USB like a flash drive.

Action camera review
One of the biggest issues with this camera is that it’s not 4K, despite some marketing claims. It also lacks any kind of stabilization. Just walking around with it causes a lot of shaking and stuttering — so much so that even video editing software struggles to smooth it out.

Another problem is the autofocus. During any quick or abrupt movement, the image goes out of focus, which makes the footage unusable if you’re trying to film anything dynamic.

If you’re looking for a basic body cam for documentation or evidence purposes, it might do the job. But if you’re planning to use it for content creation, like YouTube videos, it’s probably not a good choice.

The app experience was also disappointing. I use an iPhone, and the app they ask you to download appears to be a generic dash cam app. It connected to the camera, but I couldn’t access the camera feed or control it through the app.

Ok depending on your usage style
I bought this to film my competition runs which last about 30 secs each at best. What I learned is that if you are going to use this to stop/start filming often, DO NOT connect it to the app at all or use the because it will interfere with your ability to stop/start filming regularly. Start it, hit record, stop, record, stop, etc. as many times as needed, but dont connect to the app until you are done completely. Oh also, it will auto shut off after like 5 mins so keep that in mind too if you are filming intermittently like I am. If you do connect to the app while filming, you will need to reset it in order to get that easy start/stop functionality working again. This is ONLY if you are using the physical button on the camera to record. If you are using the app to do so, then you will probably be fine.

The connection to the app is unreliable though so it’s easier to use this camera without it.

Other than that the camera is solid enough for the price. Didn’t include a hat clip, which in all fairness, isn’t shown to be included so I had to buy one separately.

Nifty camera for cycling
I had been looking for a lightweight, 1080p or 4K camera to replace a camera I already had, but was too heavy and bulky.

After weeks of searching, I came across this mini camera, and it’s good reviews. I finally ordered it to give it a try.

Out of the box it’s very lightweight, and is truly the size of my thumb. It comes with a variety of adapters for mounting, and a microSD card.

After fully charging it (the light turns off when fully charged) I took it for a test spin on the bike. The mounting location I chose was the magnetic necklace. Later, when looking at the footage, I found the camera angle was too low and the only ‘footage’ was my handlebar and arms.

The next day I tried another location. This time I used the frame, and zip tied it to my Garmin mount. Not bad. I liked the spot, but the camera moved too much. Also, because I had the strap attached to the camera, it became a pain to constantly tie and untie the strap.

The third ride had me take the helmet mount and glue it to the front of my helmet. I then hide the strap inside the helmet so if the mount comes loose, I won’t lose the camera. This worked, AND the footage is awesome.

Good but zero stabilization.
Giving it a 5 star for price and all the goodies it comes with but the camera really lacks in stabilization, so 4 star total. So if you plan to use it for active work like biking or hiking, it's good. But if it's stable and not moving it's pretty good quality! Just lacks stabilization. Which is what I need.

It’s good enough, but with a lot of caveats.
Good. Could not connect to the app despite all my best attempts and troubleshooting. I read the instructions thoroughly. Didn’t come with a head strap or any other way to strap it to the body while underwater. You can hold it, but that makes it hard to swim. The waterproof case itself does work well. The only button is sensitive and the design (long press for certain functions, short click for other functions) makes it difficult to know whether it is recording, especially underwater. I went for long periods of time thinking I was recording but I was not recording. Also it went through a phase where I could not get it to record; it would only go into the “seeking wifi / app connection” mode instead of recording (it blinks and vibrates differently in this mode). Honestly the idea of having the device vibrate to communicate what mode it is in is a bad idea because you can’t sense vibrations well at all when you are underwater and the water is moving a lot around you. The vibrations are, however, easier to detect when holding the device on dry land.
Also the attachments that the camera comes with do seem like good build quality, BUT they are not at all intuitive to use. There are not instructions for how to use them or what they are for. Half the plastic attachments, I don’t know what they are for or how to use them.

Since I can’t connect to the app, I just use an SD card reader to get the video files from the camera.
I also wish the video files weren’t required to be broken up into 3-minute files. I wish I could just have one long video for each recording session. But there is no option for me to change this (at least not without the app). The app itself, by the way, is poorly designed, and the section for tech support said (in poor English) that it could not accept contacts at this time – so there is no tech support.

The battery life was not quite what I expected, maybe more like an hour rather than three hours. I did not time it so I am estimating.

Overall I don’t regret getting this camera but it did disappoint me a good bit.

Simple inexpensive travel camera
Took this on a weekend trip and it’s kind of perfect for travel. It’s genuinely thumb-sized, super light, and the one-button design means I can start recording while I’m walking out the door. The magnetic pendant + safety cord makes it easy to wear under a hoodie or jacket so the camera sits neatly on top of your outer layer - hands-free for hikes, city walks, or grabbing coffee. The 360 degree back clip is great on a backpack strap or hat brim, so I can switch angles fast without digging in my bag. It’s small enough to be discreet in museums or shops where filming is allowed (always follow the rules), and it looks clean and professional for quick recap clips at the end of the day.

At some point during the trip I had a brainwave, the magnet opens up creative mounts: stick it to a metal railing for a quick time-lapse, park it on a rental car’s metal surface (stationary) for a packing shot, or pop it on a hotel lamp base for a room tour.

And because it comes with a 128GB card, I didn’t have to think about storage - just charge it with a power bank via USB and keep going, ready out of the box.

One note: there’s no built-in image stabilization. If you’re moving fast, footage can look a bit shaky. I got the best results by clipping it to a stable surface (backpack strap is better than hand-holding), keeping my steps smooth, and letting it sit still for panoramas or time-lapses.

Fun, simple bodycamera
Happy with this purchase. It's a fun, simple, inexpensive body camera. I've used it mainly for baseball games to do a "coach-cam" and on vacation recording family outings.  It's a high enough resolution that you can zoom in when editing and capture plays further out in the field. The magnet backing is strong, so it's stayed on me while mountain biking, kayaking, umpiring, and hiking.  The button controls are very easy to turn it on, start recording, turn it off, etc.  I found it much easier to use the button controls than the app.  The audio quality is pretty good, although with windy days it can struggle.

The app itself was not user friendly and I chose not to use it. It took a while to understand that the camera creates a wi-fi network, and the phone has to connect to it in order to access the controls. Then, when recording, the phone screen has to stay on while recording. This didn't work during baseball and other activities. It's a bit tedious to connect the cam to a laptop and download the videos but manageable.

For the price, this camera is worth it.

Great accessories but issues in low light and lacks image stabilization
So it's a good camera for the price, but it's lacking in image stabilization which is a huge issue since this type of camera is meant to be strapped down to something like a helmet when doing activities.

In addition to that, it performs quite poorly in low light situations. For sure this camera is meant to be used outside, but in anything except direct sunlight, the quality of the video is reduced. Underwater this can be problematic.

Now the good: it's really inexpensive. You get camera + a ton of accessories including a hard case for essentially what is the cost of only the accessories for a GoPro, no camera. Its TINY, really small and one of the smallest I've seen at this price which is fantastic.

Who's it for: someone who does mainly outdoor activities, who values a small form factor camera to squeeze somewhere, doesn't like getting spending a lot on action cameras, and who doesn't mind the lack of image stabilization as they can keep the camera reasonably steady.

Overall, it’s a reliable option for casual users looking for an affordable action camera with decent performance.

It does what it is intended to do, and that is record said 2K quality footage. Despite no built-in stabilization, it does this basic function well enough, though really not much else.

This is not necessarily a professional design even though it is compact, and does appear to be similar in appearance to a larger brand name. It also comes with a lot of accessories, including the neck mag clip which is useful and helps with stabilization a little bit, but is a very basic fix.

Overall it is nothing special, but hey, it will get the job done at an affordable range by comparison to the larger brands, which is why I chose it. 4 stars for what it is.

The battery lasts pretty long, I would guess about three hours of continuous recording. I took I tout for a 1.5 hour dog walk and it recorded the whole thing, and ended up with 30% battery remaining.

A lack of image stabilization makes this a difficult camera for use in high-motion situations like sports. I could see this being useful iff you have a job as a security person or bouncer where having some interactions recorded would be beneficial.

Poor quality video but it works
Oh boy. For the relatively speaking meagre price of $80 you get a really kitted out action camera. But as one can expect, the quality of the video leaves a lot to be desired. While it does technically output in 1440p, the video quality is quite poor. The center of the image is pretty sharp and the sides are a little mushy; not surprising considering how wide the field of view is though. Colors are a bit weird and there's of course no stabilization in a product at this price range. The main drawback is that the camera has trouble handling exposure, so there's often a lot of fully blown out parts to the images. The microphones do work (mono) but don't offer that much clarity either, though I suspect this wouldn't be the biggest requirement for this product segment.

The overall video is serviceable but won't win any prizes at all. Compared to a proper camera the video size I suppose isn't the worst, getting roughly 230mb for a 3 minute 2560x1440 video, but thankfully this also comes with a 128GB microSD card.

I think in general the best way to describe this is that it's a well kitted out action camera when it comes to the accessories, with serviceable video that can be used to capture some memories but not in a very pretty way. For the price, you can do far worse. But if you want something you can properly use and share on social media with your friends, you might want to get something better.

decently priced, but ensure you are okay with the short battery life
This mini action camera from Losfom is also sold by another brand as a mini body camera. This action is much small than other models. In fact, this body cam is smaller than most wireless earbud cases. With the reduced size, you do give up some regular action camera features like longer battery life, display, adjustable camera angles, etc. This camera does retain a nice camera, clip mount, magnetic mount, and a 128 Gb MicroSD card preinstalled. Do note that the neck strap is adjustable but not removable. They also include a MicroSD to USB A reader and a USB C to USB A adapter, but I usually use a USB hub so I have no need for these. I believe the camera itself isn't waterproof, but Losfom says the included enclosure will allow this camera to submerge to 30 M. They did not provide an IP rating, so you will just have to trust them. In actual use, this camera will record for about an hour and a half which is decent but not great. The lack of buttons also means setting the date is a bit of a pain, especially if you don't have access to a laptop. Full featured action cameras sets the time and date by GPS, so you may just remove the time tag altogether. The video quality is okay but does not compete with most modern smartphones with OIS camera lens. This mini camera is most suitable for people who likes the small form factor and do not mind that some features are absent.

Fun lil' camera that shoots Ultra HD (3840x2160)
Once you open it and charge the camera for a bit, it's pretty easy to get up and running. Long push to turn it on/off, short push to start/stop recording (blinking blue light.)

Once recording, it will continue until you stop or it dies (it will give you a long vibration warning when it needs to be recharged.) To prevent oversized files, your videos are saved as 5-minute clips, with each file approximately 320 MB in size. The camera comes with a 128gb storage card, so if the math is correct, you could fit approximately 410 video files on the 128 GB card, and the total length of those videos would be about 2,050 minutes, or roughly 34.17 hours of footage.

I do a lot of film-related shenanigans, so it was a little confusing when this says it's "4k" but the box says it's 2k... When I import my footage into DaVinci Resolve, the specs say each clip is 3840x2160 Ultra HD. It doesn't compare to the camera quality of a Blackmagic, but it's about the same as a GoPro, & again, comes with all the same accessories to mount to just about anything. I especially enjoyed the magnetic lanyard, allowing the camera to mount on your chest like a bodycam.

I'm primarily taking videos of dogs and gardening and my arms and body are always moving so I'm having difficulty finding a good spot on my person for the camera to nest and get the best framing. Efficace, même sans parler – les gens se tiennent à carreaux dès qu’ils la voientQue
J’ai finalement utilisé la caméra corporelle SHPTDJTIC 4K dans plusieurs situations, notamment à vélo et en trottinette, et franchement, elle change la donne.

Ce qui m’a le plus surpris ? C’est l’effet dissuasif immédiat. Dès que les automobilistes ou les passants repèrent la mini dashcam accrochée à la veste ou au sac, les comportements changent du tout au tout. Moins de coups de klaxon agressifs, plus de distance de sécurité, les dépassements deviennent soudainement plus prudents… comme par magie.

Techniquement, elle tient la route :
 • L’image est vraiment propre en 4K, même en mouvement.
 • Le champ de vision est large, ce qui permet de capter tout ce qu’il se passe autour.
 • Le son est clair.
 • Et surtout, le bouton d’enregistrement rapide est très rassurant à l’usage : on appuie, ça filme, pas besoin de se poser de questions.

Elle n’est pas trop lourde, passe presque inaperçue, sauf quand on veut qu’elle se voie. Et c’est là qu’elle est la plus utile.

En résumé :
Ce n’est pas juste une caméra. C’est une présence silencieuse mais visible, qui incite naturellement à la prudence autour de vous. Et ça, c’est presque aussi précieux que les images qu’elle enregistre

Une caméra petite et performante
Cette petite caméra ne paie pas de mine, mais fournit des vidéos de qualité.

Elle est petite, à peine plus grosse que mon pouce, et est livrée avec pleins d'accessoires pour pouvoir filmer dans pleins de situations : autour du cou avec un collier aimanté quand on se promène, sur un vélo avec une coque qu'on peut ensuite fixer à un support qui va sur le guidon... Les utilisations sont multiples et c'est vraiment pratique.

La qualité des vidéos enregistrées est de bonne qualité. C'est ce qui m'a le plus impressionné avec une caméra de cette gamme.

On peut visualiser les vidéos grâce à l'application dédiée, qui fonctionne, mais qui est un peu austère et mal traduite...
L'application permet aussi d'accéder aux réglages de l'appareil, ce qui est nécessaire car la caméra en elle-même ne possède qu'un bouton qui permet de lancer un enregistrement ou, au moment du paramétrage, de s'y connecter en wifi.

Caméra discrète, pratique et efficace
Caméra minuscule, mais qui peut enregistrer en 4K avec un rendu assez net, même quand on est en mouvement. Je l’ai utilisée en balade à vélo, attachée à la bretelle de mon sac grâce au clip rotatif, et aussi testée sur un support métallique avec l’aimant (sur le cadre d'une moto, juste pour voir). Ça tient bien dans les deux cas, même si sur des surfaces un peu arrondies, le magnétisme montre ses limites.

L’image est propre pour une aussi petite caméra. Les couleurs sont équilibrées en plein jour, le grand-angle donne une bonne impression d’immersion, et il y a une forme de stabilisation intégrée qui adoucit un peu les secousses. Elle chauffe légèrement au bout d’un moment, mais rien d’alarmant, même après une session d’une heure.

L’autonomie tourne autour de deux heures sans recharge, si on veut filmer plus longtemps, il faut la brancher à une batterie externe (testée aussi, ça marche). La caméra enregistre par défaut en clips de 3 minutes. C’est pratique pour la gestion des fichiers, mais un peu contraignant si on veut un seul long plan sans montage. On peut modifier ça via un fichier texte, mais c’est un peu archaïque comme méthode, d'autres paramètres sont modifiables de la même manière.

Le Wi-Fi intégré permet de transférer les vidéos vers le téléphone tant qu’on reste à moins de 10 mètres. Par contre, dès qu’on s’éloigne, la prévisualisation coupe, même si l’enregistrement continue.

Bref, cette petite caméra est bien pensée pour capter des moments sur le vif sans s’encombrer. Elle s’adapte facilement à différents supports, l’image est bonne, et elle reste simple à utiliser malgré quelques réglages via fichier texte.

Cons:

A great camera considering the price point
Considering the price of this body cam / action cam I really didn't expect a whole lot from the unit. Not only in features or usefulness but most importantly not a lot in quality of the video.

But I was totally wrong, and it's pretty amazing how good the video and photo quality is as well as the quality of the camera itself as well as the accessories and the usefulness of it. all. Don't get me wrong - it isn't I the same category as the big boys when it comes to action cams but it is quite good for the price.

I guess I'll start with a few of the negatives though.

The single button on the camera works fine, especially once you get used to the ends and outs of either tapping it or holding it for a bit, depending on  what function you are using. But it really should either start recording or have an option to start recording as soon as it's turned on, rather than this being accomplished with two button presses instead of one (one longer press to turn it on, another to start recording - not that hard though). Reading this over - this may be a bit nitpicky.

And there's no way to take photos with the button itself, you do have to use the app while the camera is connected to take any photos. You may or may not care about the ability to take photos via the button. If you have the camera resolution set to the maximum (12mp) then you get the same resolution as the HD quality video anyway, so maybe you need the ability to take photo while it is in standalone mode, or not - but you can always grab a still from the video itself no matter what.

The app itself is okay, it does the job and after a few minutes of messing around it's pretty easy to work out. You do have to connect directly to the camera's Wi-Fi to do anything while using the app, and it does go through the process fairly quickly once you have it set up. It would be a little awkward for some people the first time around, I suppose.

So onto the camera itself and the stuff I liked about it.

Camera looks great, really nice design, well-manufactured housing and wow; it looks real pro quality in general. The plastic is solid and well-fitting.

The side has a little plastic/rubber water-resistant cover for the SD card and the USB-c charger. You can also use the camera as a webcam while connected to this USB-c port. They included a USB cable of moderate length.

It came with SD card already inserted and formatted, 64 gb.

In addition to the SD card port and USB-c port, there is a small push button that can be held down to reset the camera .

The USB-c also is your computer interface if you want to connect directly to the camera for downloading video or photos or using it as a webcam or charging it, and the nice thing here is that you can charge the unit while you're recording. Which means you could power it with a power bank or something like that for more recording time, though you might look into a 90-degree USB-C connector so it's not sticking out of the side. The light on the front changes color when it is fully charged.

One of the best features is that the camera is very small and fairly unobtrusive. As it says in the description on Amazon; it is about the size of your thumb. Possibly in some occasions you might want to cover the blue flashing light with a bit of electrical tape to really hide it.

The cover around the lens' cover protrudes out from the body, slightly providing a little protection for the lens cover itself, and I suppose I might say this is a bit of a negative as it could possibly be more susceptible to scratching as the lens cover is very very close to the level of that ridge protecting it. It would be nice if it were more indented a bit, but that would add some size to the overall unit I suppose.

To power on the camera you hold the single button down for a short time and the blue light comes and it vibrates briefly to show that it's powered on and in standby. Having that vibration function - which is nice - allow you can go through the camera's functions without actually looking at the light if you feel for that vibration indication.

Once powered on it is in standby and eventually powers down after about 3 minutes, if nothing else is done with it.

To start recording you press this button again briefly, the camera vibrates again to tell you that it's now recording and the blue light slowly flashes.

To stop it you just press the button again and to shut it down you hold the button down a little longer. Very very easy.

To connect to the app make sure the camera is turned on and in standby mode and then press the button twice quickly and it switches to Wi-Fi mode, with the green light flashing quickly, then you just follow the prompt in the app for connecting.

When the app is connected to the camera, you can set the resolution for the recording mode, with video options that go from a low resolution through HD and up to 4K with the maximum resolution of 2840x2160. The lens is a bit of a wide angle range and video is saved in pretty standard 16:9 MP4 30fps video format.

There are a few other options, all pretty basic and straightforward stuff like being able to adjust the exposure level, and other options, etc.

The video itself is pretty good, I would say medium-quality and everything is fairly clear and identifiable. I mean it's obviously not the absolute highest quality like some of the real expensive action cams, but it's a cut above some of the other moderate-priced ones that I have and certainly much better than any real cheap ones I've tried. Some of which are nearly unusable.

And it also does record audio fairly well and from up to a medium distance. The microphone is right under the lens, and seemingly also quite wide angle in recording.

Battery life is rated at 2 hours and I suppose I'm getting about this. But I'm using mostly medium resolution and I haven't really tested at the highest resolution for a long period of time, but it is a very good battery life for such a tiny camera.

There is no mention of image stabilization, I do not believe that it has it.

For setting the options of course you need to connect the app, using the camera's Wi-Fi and then you have access to things like resolution and setting the time and date and also seeing the video in real time. And the ability to take pictures as well as video and downloading the pictures and photos.

It records on a loop and you should be able to set the interval but for some reason the interval setting does not seem to do anything. The loop will overwrite older video when the SD card fills up, there is no other option that I can see.

One thing to note here is that when you are previewing the video and pictures these are just medium quality and just for preview, and when you want to actually put them on your phone and save them in full resolution then you must transfer them from the app into your album via the download. Or you can plug the camera into your computer via the USB-c or take the SD card out and access it.

Speaking of that, one of the accessories they give you is a USB dongle that has an SD/TF card reader on the end of it in case this is the route you undertake for downloading photos and videos. Though it works just fine through the phone or via the USB-c cable.

You can actually manually set the date and time by plugging it into a computer via the USB-c port and accessing a file called time and its root directory and setting the time and date and that. Possibly a holdover from the days before there was a connected app.

So theoretically you could use this without ever even connecting it to an app.

There's also a quick instruction booklet for getting you started on button presses and setting up the app and things like that. By the way the app does come in Android and iOS versions, which you can easily access by searching for Viidure or using the QR code in the instructions.

The camera itself seems fairly weather resistant with the rubber cover over the ports but there is also a totally waterproof enclosure, capable of protecting it down to 30 meters deep. This is a really nice quality waterproof enclosure with its own waterproof button and a really good looking seal and clamp system. It even comes with its own little instructions.

If you're going to use this as a body cam or action cam for personal use and not on a vehicle or other device then probably one of the first things you want to investigate is the lanyard and magnetic base that you wear under your shirt. The back of the camera is magnetic so this pretty securely holds this through the cloth of your shirt. So far this has worked really really well as it is a very strong magnet.

Of course, with thicker clothes you'll need to use another option. But this is kind of cool in that it just kind of hovers on the front of your shirt. And while it's very strong I guess in general if I was doing some sort of very active exercise or other function then I might be worried about accidentally brushing the camera off. It would take some pressure for this to happen.

One of my favorite mounts that came with this is a clip mount that the camera just snaps into. This works if you have too thick of a jacket or something that the magnetic lanyard wouldn't hold a camera through or if you're afraid that the camera might get brushed or knocked off the magnetic mount.

The top of the camera has little indentations so that this clip-on mount securely grabs onto it when it's snapped in, and the clip action itself is very strong. And in addition the camera can be rotated through 360° so you can clip it at any angle you want, whether it's on your shirt or in a pocket or on your backpack or even maybe a hat.

Next there is an outer ringed enclosure that holds the camera securely and which has the GoPro mount on one end. Which means it can be mounted in this ringed enclosure in either direction, depending on whether you want the mount to be at the top or the bottom of the camera. This makes the camera completely compatible with GoPro mounting of pretty much any sort.

There are multiple GopRo compatible mounts included, including a clip-in one for equipment and clothing and backpacks that have the clip-in socket buckle and also it can slip into either of the two included stick-on buckles/adapters. Very strong tape is included for these. There are two receptacles and the double-sided tape pieces for them. There is also a 360 adjustable mount that fits in between any GoPro mount and the camera ring that holds it, so the camera can be positioned pointing in any direction.

Next there is also a rubbery enclosure with a strap, and an extra strap. Good for wearing around an arm or around other things of various sizes. I'm not exactly sure how the extra strap works.

Lastly and a bit of a negative, there is a GoPro bike handlebar mount, that can also attach to anything that has a comparable thickness for it to fit around like handholds, other sort of handlebars, etc.

This is adjustable but not very widely. It does have a rubber spacer that can be taken out if needed but even taking that out - it's not wide enough to fit around most bike handlebars. For smaller things it should be fine.

For my bike test video sample I had to use a GoPro handlebar mount that I had from previous cameras and accessories.

My final and current overall opinion about this camera is that it does record a good medium quality video and photo, the camera looks real good and has a ton of nice and useful accessories and the basic options that you would need. Don't expect the highest quality records, but expect a good average quality in general. The camera is a good little multi-purpose unit and is a very good item and well worth the low price for it.

Versatile little cam that can be used as a nanny cam, webcam or body cam!
For the price, there are similar options out there, but this one is the PORTABLE cam that you wear like a body cam!
My next step is to mount it on my car's mirror to record the road (you never know!) as I'm driving, just in case.
It can be used as a webcam as well, but that's not my main purpose of this little camera.
I don't like the fact that there's a blinking light on my chest as I'm recording my day, so I covered it with electrical tape to be more inconspicuous. Keep in mind all-day wear requires an external battery bank hook-up.
It does get a little hot when in use, but I'm assuming that's expected with a cam of this sort.
Setup was simple - a thorough read of the instructions was fairly explanatory.
I was truly impressed with the picture quality! At 4k, the clarity is appreciated. I do have the option to adjust the quality of video to produce smaller-size video snippets. The 3-minute default of video clips can be adjusted, but I'm OK with the shorter segments, which are numbered in order on the RF card (mini-SD card).
It can also function as a nanny cam, or mini spy cam, if you desire as well.
As long as I'm within about 30 feet of the camera, I can view what I'm recording on the phone app so it's important to stay within a pretty close proximity to the small cam.
There are mounting accessories included and even a cool clip which can serve multiple purposes with this cam for mounting.
For the price, this appears to be a good value considering there are power bank cams in the similar price range.
Battery life is as expected: 2-3 hours, unless I choose to keep my portable power bank in my pocket and hooked up to it for continuous recording and connectivity.
I'll tinker around with it to get myself familiarized a bit, but first impressions are quite good, in my opinion!

After testing quite a few body cameras, this little powerhouse genuinely surprised me. Great video quality, nice and compact. Mostly reliable.
Pros:
-Great video quality during the day time. The app includes the option to change the video resolution from 2160p, 1440p @ 30 FPS, or 1080 @ 30 FPS. Even if the the camera is clipped onto my waistband, there's some stabilization in the video which results in less shakiness.
-The small and compact profile of this body camera makes it really easy to carry around.
-Very easy to use with strong vibration feedback. I can rely on the vibrations of the device to let me know that the action I want was successfully carried out. Turning it on or off results in a long vibration. A short vibration means the video recording has started or stopped. There's also a blue light on the device which is a good indicator as to what the device is doing.
-Easy to connect to the phone. When the device is on, I just have to double-click the button to get the camera to start broadcasting a wifi access point for my phone to connect to. From there, I can change the settings on the camera or download clips to my phone. The connection and responsiveness between my phone and the camera is pretty fast. I can even view the saved clips from the camera, before downloading them.
-The device auto-shuts off after a few minutes of inactivity, if not recording. This is nice to conserve battery. However, I would make sure that the device is off.
-Good battery life with a USB-C charging port.

Cons:
-When I connect the camera to my phone, the camera always starts a new recording. There's no option in the settings to disable this.
-There's an option to change the video loop duration from the default of 3 minutes to between 1 minute to 9 minutes. Needs an Infinite Recording Option
The first thing I noticed about this camera is the size. It's REALLY compact! That makes things easier when you wearing it as it doesn't look obvious like the police cameras that are kind of a chunky box on their chest. The image on the box is WAY bigger than the actual device. I like the resolution - 4K is where it's at these days. It comes with a 64GB memory card included, which should be plenty of space for your recordings, and if you need more recording time, you can adjust the resolution you record in through the app "Viidure".
The app defaults the camera to 1080p, so if you really want 4K video, you'll need to change it through the app. The app is critical to setting the functions of the camera, and it connects via WiFi. It doesn't look like you can be on the internet while you're trying to change the configuration of the camera because you have to connect to the device's wifi hotspot, which is kind of a pain. It would be MUCH better if you could just connect via Bluetooth so you could have your wifi on and using the internet while you're using the app. The app isn't very good, so you'll probably struggle with it like I did.
Connecting to the computer via the included USB-C cable, you're able to download the files from the inserted SD card that comes with it. If you want to add a date/time stamp to the video or images, you have to edit a text document at the root of the inserted memory card, which is a little odd. You then have to set the date and time on the text file and change a character from N to Y, and then it will start recording your video with the date/time stamp. The one thing that's truly odd about this thing is that in that text file, the instructions say that you set the number of minutes you record when you press the button on the front of the device. This setting can also be changed in the app, but if you want to capture something immediately or continue capturing while you're walking around, you're a slave to that setting unless you want to logon to the wifi connection of the camera. You would think pressing the button once and then again to stop recording would be a possibility, but it doesn't look like it. Some cameras allow you to record to the disk like a minute leading up to the button press, but this device doesn't allow that. So you MUST be in the clip record mode for it to start saving, and there's a hard limit as to the length of the recording. Your subject takes 3min and 1 second to get to what you're trying to record? TOO BAD. You'll have to record a second time, thus, this bodycam is way too finicky for my liking. The maximum is 9 minutes, so you can't record your entire nature walk, only 9 minute clips that requires you to press the button for every clip you want to create.
If they improve the product with bluetooth, add infinite recording time, and improve their app, then I would change the rating, but right now, it's too fussy to do much with this thing. I really like the size (super small), I like the accessories they give you, I always love when companies give you a USB-C port on it, and the video looks decent. But the negatives overwhelm the positives, unfortunately.

Nice package, but the image stabilization isn't good.
Let's start with the positives: This is a great little package, with an easy to use camera and multiple convenient accessories.  There are lots of options on how you mount or use this camera.  The simplist "magnetic" backing, with the magnet tucked under a layer, is super easy, flexible.  There are numerous other ways to go that I haven't played with yet, but... it comes pretty complete.
Second positive: It's been pretty easy to jump right in and use.  The interface is super simple and you can start recording quickly and easily.  I downloaded the app to my iPhone and the camera connected to my phone easily, giving me nice quick access to my videos.  I also connected the camera to my desktop Mac without a problem.  I was able to review videos, delete a few I knew I didn't need, and upload what I did want to an editing program.
So what's the problem?  Stabilization.  It's awful.  Hey, you get what you pay for, but if you record while moving (sort of the point of an action camera), the footage can be close to unwatchable.  I've searched for a stabilization setting (can't find it) and I emailed the company (they didn't respond to the first email a few months ago, just sent another tonight).  I've down loaded a third party stabilization software that I've played with some.  We'll see is we can make the image better.

Bottomline - Great price, nice package, good accessories, but damn... the video quality... and that's why you buy a camera, right?

Good product
I've been testing this 4K Mini Action Camera for a few weeks now, using it for cycling, playing with my dog, and even some casual vlogging. Here’s my honest breakdown of its strengths and weaknesses.
First Impressions & Design (5/5)
Right out of the box, this camera lives up to its "mini" and "thumb" POV name. It's incredibly small, lightweight, and feels surprisingly well-built for its price. The black matte finish is sleek and doesn't feel cheap. The included magnetic mount is genius it's super secure and makes switching between my bike frame, helmet, and just holding it in my hand a total breeze. The package is very generous, with a helmet mount, various adhesives, and a waterproof case that feels robust.
Video & Image Quality (3.5/5)
This is where you have to manage your expectations. For the price, it's impressive, but it's not going to compete with a GoPro or DJI Osmo Action.
• 4K Resolution: It does shoot in 4K, but the image is noticeably softer and lacks the fine detail and dynamic range of high-end action cameras. In bright, sunny conditions, the footage is very usable for social media or personal memories. Colors are decent but can sometimes look a bit oversaturated.
• 1080p Mode: This is actually the sweet spot. The video is sharper and more stable, and the file sizes are much more manageable. The 60fps mode is great for capturing smooth motion.
• Low Light Performance: This is its biggest weakness, as with most budget cameras. Footage becomes grainy and muddy in anything but ideal lighting. It's best used during the day or in well-lit environments.
Key Features & Performance
• POV (Point of View) & Portability (5/5): This is the camera's greatest strength. Its tiny, thumb-sized design makes perfect for true first-person perspective shots. Clipping it to a shirt collar, a bike helmet, or even holding it between your fingers is effortless. It's the most portable camera I've ever used.
• Waterproofing (IPX7) (4/5): The IPX7 rating means it can be submerged in 1 meter of water for 30 minutes. I tested it in the rain and a swimming pool, and it held up perfectly inside its waterproof case. It's excellent for snorkeling or rainy bike rides.
• Magnetic Mount (5/5): A standout feature! The magnet is strong and reliable. I could confidently attach it to my bike or a metal surface without fear of it falling off. It makes the camera incredibly versatile on the go.
• 128GB Included (5/5): The fact that it comes with a massive 128GB microSD card is a huge value-add. You can record hours upon hours of footage without worrying about storage right away.
• Battery Life (3/5): This is a common trade-off for such a small device. You'll get about 60-90 minutes of continuous recording in 4K. For long bike rides or vlogging sessions, you'll definitely need to carry a power bank or spare batteries (it's recommended to buy an extra battery).
Who Is This For?
• Perfect For: Cyclists, motorcyclists, skaters looking for a sleek helmet cam.
• Perfect For: Pet owners who want a lightweight camera to attach to a collar or harness.
• Perfect For: Travel vloggers or content creators who need a ultra-portable, discreet second-angle camera.
• Perfect For: Anyone on a tight budget who wants to dip their toes into action cameras without a huge investment.
Not Ideal For:
• Professional filmmakers or those needing crystal-clear, cinematic-quality footage.
• Anyone needing long battery life for all-day events without frequent charging.
• Low-light videography.
The Verdict: Excellent Value with Understood Limitations
Overall Rating: 4/5
The 4K Mini Action Camera is a fantastic little device that punches well above its weight class in terms of portability and included accessories. The magnetic mount and true POV design are game changers for capturing unique angles.
You're buying this for its convenience and fun factor, not for its absolute image quality. If you understand that you're getting a very capable, versatile, and incredibly portable camera for a fraction of the cost of a premium brand with the expected compromises in battery and low-light performance then you will be absolutely thrilled with this purchase. It's a brilliant gadget for adventurers, pet lovers, and casual creators.

Solid Little Action Cam That Packs a Punch for Water Fun
Picked this up for some underwater shots and quick vlogs on my dives and hikes, and it delivers where it counts without breaking the bank. The video comes out sharp in 4K, especially in bright light like when you're snorkeling with fish darting around, and that wide angle lens grabs everything without feeling distorted. Build feels sturdy for its tiny size, like it can take a few knocks on a rocky trail or splash without flinching, thanks to the IPX7 rating that lets you submerge it up to 10 meters no case needed. What really stands out is the bundle of extras, mounts for helmets, chests, even a magnetic clip that sticks anywhere, plus that 128GB card right out of the box, so you're set to record hours without fussing over storage or buying add ons. Makes setup a breeze for spontaneous clips of biking through mud or pet antics at the park.

That said, the built in mic picks up a lot of wind noise, so if you're serious about vlogging, pair it with an external recorder to avoid that tinny audio ruining your narration. Also, without electronic image stabilization, footage gets a bit shaky on rough rides unless you mount it steady, which might bug you if smooth pans are your thing. Still, for the price and portability, the quality punch and accessory kit make it a no brainer for casual adventurers like me who want reliable captures without the bulk of bigger cams.

A Practical Little Body Camera for Everyday Use
I’ve been using this body camera for a few weeks now, and honestly, it’s exactly what I needed — even if it’s not perfect. If you’re expecting crystal-clear cinematic footage, you’ll want to look elsewhere, but for what this camera is designed to do — discreetly record whenever you need it — it’s spot on.

First off, this thing is tiny. It’s smaller than my thumb and super lightweight, so after clipping it onto my shirt or jacket, I pretty much forget it’s even there. That’s honestly one of the biggest selling points for me. It’s not bulky or awkward like some other body cameras I’ve tried. Whether I’m walking the dog, biking through town, or just running errands, I can wear it all day without feeling like I have a gadget strapped to me.

The video quality is good enough. I mean, let’s be real — it’s not professional-grade footage. Even though it says 1080P, the image kind of reminds me of video from an older smartphone. It’s a bit soft and can get a little grainy if you’re shooting indoors or in low light. But for everyday stuff — capturing weird situations on the road, recording bike rides, documenting interactions, or just getting casual footage — it does the job. If you keep your expectations realistic, you won’t be disappointed.

The accessories it comes with are surprisingly useful. I mainly use the magnetic clip to stick it onto my jacket or backpack strap, and it holds pretty well. The 360° rotating clip is nice too if you want to tweak the angle a bit. I even tried wrapping it around my dog’s collar with the silicone band just to mess around — got some hilarious “dog’s-eye-view” footage out of it.

The battery life has been decent for me. I usually get about 2 hours of recording before needing to recharge, which is more than enough for my bike rides or quick trips around town. It charges pretty fast, and because it’s so small, it doesn’t feel like a hassle to plug in and top off when needed. You can just click to record and then stop when you want. I think thats the main aim here

One thing to point out is that there’s no screen on the camera itself. You have to either connect it to the app or just trust that it’s recording. I didn’t mind that because the app connection was pretty easy to set up. Plus, downloading the videos through WiFi or plugging it straight into my computer is straightforward. I also appreciate that it comes with 64GB of storage already built in — no need to go out and buy a separate memory card.

Real-world use: this thing really shines when you just want to have a “set it and forget it” camera for unexpected moments. I wanted something small and discreet to have running in the background in case anything ever happens — whether that’s weird interactions with strangers, traffic incidents, or just capturing random funny things that happen during the day. It gives me peace of mind without having to lug around a giant camera or keep pulling out my phone.

Now, it’s not perfect. The footage isn’t amazing, and sometimes it feels like the colours can be a little washed out. Also, the magnetic attachment is strong but not immune to being bumped out of place if you’re super active. And setting the time stamp was a little more complicated than it needed to be (you have to do it manually through a text file). But honestly, for what it costs and what it offers, I can live with those minor annoyances.

Overall, I’m giving it 4 stars. Not because it’s a perfect camera — it’s definitely not — but because it’s an incredibly practical little tool. It’s affordable, easy to use, easy to wear, and reliable for capturing the kind of footage you actually need in everyday situations. It’s like an insurance policy you can clip onto your shirt. And for that reason alone, I think it’s a great buy.

Ich bin wirklich begeistert von dieser kleinen Action Cam! Die Videoqualität ist für 1080p erstaunlich gut, selbst bei Bewegung liefert sie stabile und scharfe Aufnahmen. Der 120° Weitwinkel fängt alles ein, was wichtig ist, ideal für Vlogs, Radtouren oder als Bodycam.

Besonders gefällt mir, dass sie leicht, robust und wirklich wasserdicht ist, perfekt für Outdoor-Einsätze und Unterwasseraufnahmen. Auch das WLAN-Feature funktioniert problemlos, um Videos direkt aufs Handy zu übertragen.

Ein unverzichtbarer Begleiter zum Radfahren

Jetzt kann ich es freihändig an meiner Brust oder meinem Helm befestigen, und die Bilder sind unglaublich stabil. Regentage und Strandausflüge sind kein Problem; ich kann einfach die Hülle aufsetzen und ins Wasser gehen. Die Schärfe ist für mich mehr als ausreichend. Der Akku hält über zwei Stunden, und ich lade ihn normalerweise während der Nutzung auf, ohne mir Sorgen machen zu müssen, dass ihm der Akku ausgeht. Wenn ich nach Hause komme, kann ich mir die Videos sofort auf meinem Handy ansehen und sie mit Freunden teilen. Sie alle sagen, es sieht aus, als würden sie mit mir fahren.

PROS
-lots of useful attachment and protection accessories, I find the magnetic lanyard really useful
-4K video (pretty compressed) from a nice and wide angle lens
-lightweight and quite tiny
-pretty good stabilisation (not a patch on the big-brand cameras, but this is a fraction of the price)
-can charge while recording
-USB-C charging
-decent integration with phones via their app
-decent weatherproofing

CONS
-requires odd text-based alterations to time and recording length settings (while connected to a computer)
-for a body/action camera, the battery really is a bit tiny
-not actually waterproof, unless in waterproof case
-a bit pricey, at £82.99 at the time of this review
-strange that it insists on keeping to the 3 minute video limit (no matter what settings changes I make)

NOTES
-if shooting video while plugged into USB power, be sure to stop the recording before unplugging, otherwise you’ll lose your video

un appareil tout-en-un
J’ai été agréablement surpris par cette caméra d’action Nisanuki, surtout compte tenu de son format ultra-compact. Dès la prise en main, on comprend que le produit a été pensé pour la mobilité, la simplicité et la polyvalence.
Format ultra-léger et discrétion totale
Avec seulement 35 g et une taille inférieure à celle d’un pouce, cette caméra se fait totalement oublier une fois fixée. Que ce soit sur un casque, un vélo ou simplement dans une poche, elle ne gêne absolument pas. C’est un vrai avantage pour filmer de manière naturelle, sans attirer l’attention ni se surcharger d’équipement.
Qualité vidéo 4K convaincante
La résolution 4K associée au capteur CMOS offre une image nette et détaillée, largement suffisante pour des vidéos de voyage, des vlogs ou des activités sportives. Le grand angle de 120° permet de capturer l’environnement sans avoir à se soucier du cadrage, ce qui est parfait pour une utilisation mains libres.
Utilisation simple grâce au Wi-Fi
La connexion Wi-Fi avec l’application Viidure est un vrai plus. L’aperçu en temps réel permet de vérifier le cadrage instantanément, et toutes les fonctions essentielles sont accessibles depuis le smartphone : lancement de l’enregistrement, changement de résolution, suppression des fichiers. Le partage sur les réseaux sociaux est rapide et fluide.
Étanchéité efficace jusqu’à 30 mètres
Le boîtier étanche certifié IP68 inspire confiance. Testée en piscine et en mer, la caméra fonctionne parfaitement sous l’eau. Pour la natation, le snorkeling ou le surf, elle fait clairement le travail sans compromettre la qualité d’image.
Autonomie correcte et usage continu
La batterie 800 mAh offre environ 2 heures d’enregistrement, ce qui est très correct pour une caméra aussi compacte. Le fait de pouvoir filmer tout en rechargeant est un vrai point fort, notamment pour les longues sorties à vélo ou les trajets prolongés.
Polyvalence remarquable
Les accessoires fournis permettent de l’utiliser dans de nombreux contextes :
caméra de sport
caméra de vlog
caméra corporelle
caméra pour vélo ou casque
caméra embarquée
même caméra pour animaux
C’est clairement un appareil tout-en-un, sans avoir besoin d’acheter des accessoires supplémentaires.

Compacte, Pratique et Efficace
Elle offre une bonne qualité d’image et un grand angle pratique pour capturer l’action. Compacte et légère, elle convient bien aux vlogs et aux utilisateurs qui cherchent une action cam abordable.

Preiswerte Action-Cam mit guter Leistung und vielseitigem Zubehör
Ich habe diese Action-Kamera nun getestet und bin sehr zufrieden mit den Ergebnissen. Besonders positiv ist mir aufgefallen, wie unkompliziert die Einrichtung funktioniert. Die Menüführung ist verständlich und die Kamera reagiert schnell auf Eingaben, sodass man ohne große Einarbeitung sofort loslegen kann. Zusätzlich liegt ein umfangreiches Zubehörpaket bei, mit dem sich die Kamera flexibel befestigen lässt, sei es am Fahrrad, am Helm oder an anderen Sportausrüstungen.

Die Bildqualität ist für diese Preisklasse wirklich überzeugend. Bewegungen werden flüssig aufgenommen und Farben wirken natürlich und klar. Auch Unterwasser funktioniert die Kamera zuverlässig. Das wasserdichte Gehäuse macht einen stabilen Eindruck und hat bei mir bisher dicht gehalten, selbst beim längeren Schwimmen. Der Weitwinkel erlaubt dynamische Perspektiven, die man bei Sportaufnahmen sehr gut gebrauchen kann.

Die Akkulaufzeit ist in Ordnung und reicht für typische Ausflüge oder sportliche Einsätze aus, bevor man wieder aufladen muss. Praktisch ist außerdem, dass die Kamera nicht zu groß und nicht zu schwer ist, sodass sie auch über längere Zeit nicht stört. Lediglich bei sehr schlechten Lichtverhältnissen merkt man, dass sie nicht mit teuren Profimodellen mithalten kann. Für den normalen Freizeitgebrauch ist das jedoch absolut vertretbar.

Insgesamt bietet diese Kamera ein sehr gutes Preis-Leistungs-Verhältnis. Sie ist robust, vielseitig einsetzbar und liefert ohne großen Aufwand schöne Aufnahmen. Für alle, die eine solide Action-Cam suchen, ohne ein Vermögen auszugeben, kann ich dieses Modell ohne Einschränkungen empfehlen. Volle fünf Sterne.

Bella
Ho acquistato questa fotocamera perché volevo un modello piccolo e comodo per riprendermi mentre faccio attività, sia a terra che in acqua. Dopo averlo provato, la verità è che sono abbastanza felice. Il video 4K mi sembra ottimo per questo tipo di fotocamera: i colori sono vivaci, i dettagli sono nitidi e la fluidità è il formato usuale anche se c'è molto movimento nella scena. La scelta del 120º per il grandangolo mi sembra ideale, visto che non ci si perde molto là fuori ma non si vedono nemmeno gli angoli molto storti, aspetto che di solito mi dà fastidio.
Anche il suono mi ha sorpreso, perché non mi aspettavo molto da un microfono così piccolo come quello che ha, ma penso che la qualità sia abbastanza buona. La voce è chiara e cattura bene anche l'ambiente, che è ciò che potrebbe interessarti in registrazioni più veloci o ricordi di viaggio.
Penso che il WiFi sia un'ottima funzionalità. Ti consente di associare la fotocamera al tuo cellulare e attivare la registrazione da lì con l'app o trasmettere i video senza dover rimuovere la fotocamera, il che accelera molto il processo e non ti obbliga ad avere sempre un PC.
A proposito, la custodia subacquea è un'altra delle sue virtù, l'ho bagnata in piscina e in mare e non ha avuto nemmeno una goccia d'ingresso. Oltre ad essere facile da installare, ti dà molta tranquillità quando registri sott'acqua.
Dispone inoltre di un buon numero di accessori: chiusure, cinghie e adattatori che consentono di fissarlo alla testa, alla bici o al selfie stick, ad esempio. In questo modo, è una fotocamera molto completa sin dal primo minuto.
In breve, una fotocamera leggera, che mette in difficoltà le fotocamere più pesanti e con una buona grafia nei suoi punti di forza. Molto completo per un atleta, un viaggiatore o un comunicatore quotidiano.

Ottima
Una action cam 4K di fascia media tende a offrire immagini nitide e dettagliate quando si gira con buona luce: il formato 4K davvero mostra differenza rispetto al Full HD soprattutto nei paesaggi o nei replay su schermi grandi. Tuttavia, in condizioni di luce scarsa emergono limiti: rumore digitale, colori meno brillanti e stabilizzazione spesso insufficiente. 📸
Dal punto di vista dell’autonomia, ci si può aspettare circa 1 ora o poco più in Full HD, mentre in 4K la durata cala sensibilmente. Gli accessori inclusi (custodia waterproof, supporti vari) fanno valore aggiunto, specie se ben fatti. L’usabilità è generalmente buona: interfaccia semplice, app quasi sempre supportate. Il rapporto qualità/prezzo è interessante se non si pretendono prestazioni professionali.

Buena video y micro de calidad, con muchos accesorios
Compré esta cámara porque quería un modelo pequeño y cómodo para grabarme haciendo actividades, tanto en tierra como en el agua. Después de probarla, la verdad es que estoy bastante contento. El vídeo en 4K me parece que está muy bien para este tipo de cámaras: los colores son vibrantes, se ven los detalles nítidos y la fluidez es la habitual del formato aunque haya mucho movimiento en la escena. La elección de 120 º para el gran angular me parece la ideal, ya que no te pierdes mucho por ahí pero tampoco ves las esquinas muy torcidas, que es un aspecto que me suele molestar.
El sonido también me ha sorprendido, porque no le esperaba mucho a un micrófono tan pequeño como el que lleva, pero me parece que la calidad es bastante buena. Se oye la voz clara y además capta bien el ambiente, que es lo que te puede interesar en grabaciones más rápidas o en recuerdos de viajes.
Me parece que el WiFi es una función estupenda. Te permite emparejar la cámara con el móvil y accionar la grabación desde ahí con la app o pasarte los vídeos sin tener que quitarte la cámara, lo que agiliza mucho el proceso y no te ata a disponer siempre de un PC.
Por cierto, el estuche para sumergirse es otra de sus virtudes, la he mojado en la piscina y en el mar y no ha tenido ninguna gota de entrada. Además de fácil de poner, te da muchísima tranquilidad de grabar bajo el agua.
Además cuenta con un buen número de accesorios: sujetadores, correas, y adaptadores que te permiten por ejemplo fijarla a la cabeza, a la bici o a un palo selfie. De esta manera es una cámara que desde el minuto uno es muy completa.
En definitiva, una cámara ligera, que pone en aprietos a las cámaras de más peso y con buena letra en sus puntos fuertes. Muy completa para un deportista, viajero o divulgador de lo cotidiano.

Provata fino a 10 metri, top
Questa action cam 4K è stata una piacevolissima sorpresa! La qualità video è davvero ottima: immagini nitide, colori vividi e un angolo di visuale molto ampio. L’ho usata sia in bici che al mare e ha sempre registrato senza problemi. La custodia subacquea è robusta e perfettamente impermeabile, l’ho testata fino a circa 10 metri e non ha perso una goccia.
Molto comodo anche il WiFi integrato, che permette di scaricare i video direttamente sullo smartphone. La batteria dura abbastanza e si ricarica velocemente.
Gli accessori inclusi sono tanti e utili: staffe, supporti per casco e bici, perfetti per ogni tipo di attività.
Nel complesso è una videocamera compatta, versatile e affidabile, ideale per chi ama sport, viaggi o semplicemente vuole registrare bei momenti in movimento.

Buena video y micro de calidad, con muchos accesorios
Compré esta cámara porque quería un modelo pequeño y cómodo para grabarme haciendo actividades, tanto en tierra como en el agua. Después de probarla, la verdad es que estoy bastante contento. El vídeo en 4K me parece que está muy bien para este tipo de cámaras: los colores son vibrantes, se ven los detalles nítidos y la fluidez es la habitual del formato aunque haya mucho movimiento en la escena. La elección de 120 º para el gran angular me parece la ideal, ya que no te pierdes mucho por ahí pero tampoco ves las esquinas muy torcidas, que es un aspecto que me suele molestar.
El sonido también me ha sorprendido, porque no le esperaba mucho a un micrófono tan pequeño como el que lleva, pero me parece que la calidad es bastante buena. Se oye la voz clara y además capta bien el ambiente, que es lo que te puede interesar en grabaciones más rápidas o en recuerdos de viajes.
Me parece que el WiFi es una función estupenda. Te permite emparejar la cámara con el móvil y accionar la grabación desde ahí con la app o pasarte los vídeos sin tener que quitarte la cámara, lo que agiliza mucho el proceso y no te ata a disponer siempre de un PC.
Por cierto, el estuche para sumergirse es otra de sus virtudes, la he mojado en la piscina y en el mar y no ha tenido ninguna gota de entrada. Además de fácil de poner, te da muchísima tranquilidad de grabar bajo el agua.
Además cuenta con un buen número de accesorios: sujetadores, correas, y adaptadores que te permiten por ejemplo fijarla a la cabeza, a la bici o a un palo selfie. De esta manera es una cámara que desde el minuto uno es muy completa.
En definitiva, una cámara ligera, que pone en aprietos a las cámaras de más peso y con buena letra en sus puntos fuertes. Muy completa para un deportista, viajero o divulgador de lo cotidiano.

un appareil tout-en-un
J’ai été agréablement surpris par cette caméra d’action Nisanuki, surtout compte tenu de son format ultra-compact. Dès la prise en main, on comprend que le produit a été pensé pour la mobilité, la simplicité et la polyvalence.
Format ultra-léger et discrétion totale
Avec seulement 35 g et une taille inférieure à celle d’un pouce, cette caméra se fait totalement oublier une fois fixée. Que ce soit sur un casque, un vélo ou simplement dans une poche, elle ne gêne absolument pas. C’est un vrai avantage pour filmer de manière naturelle, sans attirer l’attention ni se surcharger d’équipement.
Qualité vidéo 4K convaincante
La résolution 4K associée au capteur CMOS offre une image nette et détaillée, largement suffisante pour des vidéos de voyage, des vlogs ou des activités sportives. Le grand angle de 120° permet de capturer l’environnement sans avoir à se soucier du cadrage, ce qui est parfait pour une utilisation mains libres.
Utilisation simple grâce au Wi-Fi
La connexion Wi-Fi avec l’application Viidure est un vrai plus. L’aperçu en temps réel permet de vérifier le cadrage instantanément, et toutes les fonctions essentielles sont accessibles depuis le smartphone : lancement de l’enregistrement, changement de résolution, suppression des fichiers. Le partage sur les réseaux sociaux est rapide et fluide.
Étanchéité efficace jusqu’à 30 mètres
Le boîtier étanche certifié IP68 inspire confiance. Testée en piscine et en mer, la caméra fonctionne parfaitement sous l’eau. Pour la natation, le snorkeling ou le surf, elle fait clairement le travail sans compromettre la qualité d’image.
Autonomie correcte et usage continu
La batterie 800 mAh offre environ 2 heures d’enregistrement, ce qui est très correct pour une caméra aussi compacte. Le fait de pouvoir filmer tout en rechargeant est un vrai point fort, notamment pour les longues sorties à vélo ou les trajets prolongés.
Polyvalence remarquable
Les accessoires fournis permettent de l’utiliser dans de nombreux contextes :
caméra de sport
caméra de vlog
caméra corporelle
caméra pour vélo ou casque
caméra embarquée
même caméra pour animaux
C’est clairement un appareil tout-en-un, sans avoir besoin d’acheter des accessoires supplémentaires.

Compacte, Pratique et Efficace
Elle offre une bonne qualité d’image et un grand angle pratique pour capturer l’action. Compacte et légère, elle convient bien aux vlogs et aux utilisateurs qui cherchent une action cam abordable.

Con l’applicazione si modifica tutte le impostazioni, fa video in 4 k, molto fluide, utilizzando specialmente la calamita che mi trovo benissimo, la batteria dura in base alla qualità selezionata.

Ha molti accessori molto utili per i vari utilizzi.

Bella
Ho acquistato questa fotocamera perché volevo un modello piccolo e comodo per riprendermi mentre faccio attività, sia a terra che in acqua. Dopo averlo provato, la verità è che sono abbastanza felice. Il video 4K mi sembra ottimo per questo tipo di fotocamera: i colori sono vivaci, i dettagli sono nitidi e la fluidità è il formato usuale anche se c'è molto movimento nella scena. La scelta del 120º per il grandangolo mi sembra ideale, visto che non ci si perde molto là fuori ma non si vedono nemmeno gli angoli molto storti, aspetto che di solito mi dà fastidio.
Anche il suono mi ha sorpreso, perché non mi aspettavo molto da un microfono così piccolo come quello che ha, ma penso che la qualità sia abbastanza buona. La voce è chiara e cattura bene anche l'ambiente, che è ciò che potrebbe interessarti in registrazioni più veloci o ricordi di viaggio.
Penso che il WiFi sia un'ottima funzionalità. Ti consente di associare la fotocamera al tuo cellulare e attivare la registrazione da lì con l'app o trasmettere i video senza dover rimuovere la fotocamera, il che accelera molto il processo e non ti obbliga ad avere sempre un PC.
A proposito, la custodia subacquea è un'altra delle sue virtù, l'ho bagnata in piscina e in mare e non ha avuto nemmeno una goccia d'ingresso. Oltre ad essere facile da installare, ti dà molta tranquillità quando registri sott'acqua.
Dispone inoltre di un buon numero di accessori: chiusure, cinghie e adattatori che consentono di fissarlo alla testa, alla bici o al selfie stick, ad esempio. In questo modo, è una fotocamera molto completa sin dal primo minuto.
In breve, una fotocamera leggera, che mette in difficoltà le fotocamere più pesanti e con una buona grafia nei suoi punti di forza. Molto completo per un atleta, un viaggiatore o un comunicatore quotidiano.

Ottima
Una action cam 4K di fascia media tende a offrire immagini nitide e dettagliate quando si gira con buona luce: il formato 4K davvero mostra differenza rispetto al Full HD soprattutto nei paesaggi o nei replay su schermi grandi. Tuttavia, in condizioni di luce scarsa emergono limiti: rumore digitale, colori meno brillanti e stabilizzazione spesso insufficiente. 📸
Dal punto di vista dell’autonomia, ci si può aspettare circa 1 ora o poco più in Full HD, mentre in 4K la durata cala sensibilmente. Gli accessori inclusi (custodia waterproof, supporti vari) fanno valore aggiunto, specie se ben fatti. L’usabilità è generalmente buona: interfaccia semplice, app quasi sempre supportate. Il rapporto qualità/prezzo è interessante se non si pretendono prestazioni professionali.

Good audio recording. Video quality depends upon indoor or outdoors and positioning of camera.
Update, 10/28/2023. I have gotten some better video with this body cam, especially outside on overcast days. However, the camera slipped out of my hand at work and fell to the floor. The height of the fall was about 2 and a half feet. The small notch on the camera case that secures the two different clips broke off when the camera impacted the floor. With that notch broken off, there is no way to attach the two different clips/clamps to my clothing, to wear the camera. I ended up using an epoxy glue to secure the clips to the camera case. Unfortunately, I cannot remove that clip now.

The camera itself is well made and easy to use. It took a few attempts to learn the different menus and settings for the camera. Accessing the videos and pictures on a computer is easy. The camera is treated like a USB storage device by a computer, so the videos and photos were quickly accessible.

The quality of the audio recordings is great. The quality of the video recordings (with audio) really depends upon the setting. While recording outdoors in the sunlight, most (but not all) of my videos had a blueish tint to them. [Since I cannot post recordings of my interactions with people, I recorded different activities with this body camera.] Video recordings indoors had much better color than recordings made outdoors. An overriding factor for both types of recordings depended upon the camera's placement. Holding the camera by hand produced the best quality recordings. Recording while the camera was attached/clipped to your shirt, jacket, or belt introduce "shaking motion" into all the videos. The quality or thickness of the shirt or jacket also had an impact on the quality of the video recording. The camera is difficult to stabilize with shirts made with lighter materials. The camera easily tips either vertically or horizontally when attached to the front of a shirt, or when attached to a shirt pocket. Grasping the camera with your hand, in either of those placements, stabilizes the recordings. I had better video recordings when the camera was attached to my belt, using the larger clip included with the camera. However, in that position, the camera lens is focused on a lower field of view than a person might want.

The photos taken with the camera are poor at best, 1.3 meg. As with any photograph, lighting and settings affect the quality of the photographs. The night vision feature works, but the camera must be held closely to the subject(s) being recorded.

I also tried using the camera a makeshift "Dash Camera". It recorded my drives to and from work fairly well.

I do not like the "clips" that come with the camera used to attach the camera to clothing. There are only two ways to attach the camera to a shirt or jacket and neither function very well for recording video. The camera always tilted off the vertical when attached to the outside of a shirt pocket - as its clip is designed to attach. I kept wondering if there was a way to put the body of the camera in my shirt pocket, on either edge of the pocket, to help stabilize the camera along the vertical.

Exceptionally difficult to setup
There's lot of setup required to get this unit operational, and although I am fairly tech-savvy, it's been difficult for me. First off, the language in the User's Manual does not always match the markings on the unit, and one has to decipher which button/slot, etc. is which. For example, it would have been simpler to mark the "power" button "power", and not "video/save" with a symbol. Also, there are references to abbreviations (such as "ITI") that have not previously been defined, so it necessary to flip back & forth through the instructions trying to figure out what the abbreviations stand for. It's frustrating.

As of the writing of this review, I'm finally able to format the TF card. I'm sure I'll be able to get the camera set up eventually, but right now I need a break.

On the upside, this camera appears to be a solidly-constructed unit - that's what the 3 stars are for. I'll update this review to include ease of use and picture quality if/when I finally get it set up.

Couple of problems 📷
The 4K Mini WIFI Action Camera is a compact and portable option for capturing adventures, but it comes with several drawbacks that may hinder its usability.

While the image quality is decent, the battery life is not as impressive as advertised. It doesn’t last as long as expected, especially when recording in high quality, so be prepared for frequent recharges.

The camera is app-dependent for many functions, including tweaking settings and transferring files. While this allows for some customization, it can be frustrating if you prefer a more straightforward operation. Additionally, there’s no way to mount it to a computer, making it difficult to use as a primary camera for blogging or video editing. The lack of clear instructions on how to take photos is also a downside; the manual seems poorly written, with incomplete sentences and bad grammar that make it hard to follow.

The listing and box don’t mention that it comes with an SD card, but it turns out there is one included. However, it’s not the largest size that the company recommends, and 128GB is unlikely to last a full day of fun, even if the battery life were better.

The various mounting options work well, and the waterproof feature is a nice touch, but I experienced bubbles forming in the waterproof compartment after a drop in the water, raising concerns about its reliability in wet conditions.

Overall, this action camera has potential with its compact size and good image quality, but the battery life, app dependency, and lack of clear instructions could be improved. It’s a fair choice for casual use, but serious users might want to look for a more reliable option.

Definitely not worth 80$ to me.

A very capable mini camera for the price!
This is a surprisingly capable mini camera with pretty good image quality, a bunch of useful accessories, a magnetic back and even a microphone that isn't terrible. It's a really small camera--about the size of a thumb drive, and it comes with a 128 gigabyte micro SD card that can store quite a bit of video (accepts up to 256). Files are easily transferred to PC or Mac with an SD card reader. I did not test any of the wireless file transfer functionality as I don't need to send videos to my phone, and don't generally want to use an app if I can help it, and I have an inexpensive card reader which worked perfectly for transferring files.

This is billed as a "sports" or action camera for capturing activity, movement and so forth. There are many accessories included for mounting the camera (see video), including a waterproof case with a gasket that seals very tight, and still allows for access to the button on the front. It has a magnetic back with pretty decent holding power as well as a magnetic plate attached to a lanyard that you can put around your neck and tuck inside your shirt. Then you can stick the camera to the backer through your clothes and walk around with it hands free.

The appeal of this camera for me is that I can stick it to various steel surfaces in my workshop for filming work that I'm doing from angles I wouldn't be able to get with my phone or full-size camera. I filmed some tests for this review you can see in the video. It works great with solid picture quality and the microphone seems to work pretty well. It's not going to replace a device as capable as a reasonably current / full-featured smart phone for general filming, but for filming in tight areas or while walking around doing stuff with your hands free, it's very effective.

Picture quality seems decent for the price with a fairly crisp image. Keep in mind this is an inexpensive device compared to other action style cameras, so the colors are a bit washed out, you're not going to get fancy image stabilization and cinematic shooting modes, etc. The accessories are numerous and full featured, but not the highest quality. The capabilities for the price point represent an excellent value. It even has a pretty well written manual. Overall, I think this camera is a big win for the price.
